title.headline: CPU vs GPU Purchase Path
title.subtitle: Memory fit changes topology, and topology changes cost.

primary.label: GPU Path
primary.caption: 8 GPUs plus fabric
primary.note: Multi-GPU fit pressure sets the purchase floor.

secondary.label: CPU Path
secondary.caption: 1 RAM-heavy server
secondary.note: Single-box fit keeps the topology simple.

axis.label: Deployment spend
axis.note: Topology changes the bill before optimization begins

annotation.label: Fit first
annotation.note: If the model does not fit, peak FLOPs do not rescue the plan.

floor.note: Both paths still share the same external network realities.

thesis.title: Memory fit is a purchasing decision, not just an optimization detail.
thesis.line_1: The expensive path starts paying for topology before training or inference begins.
thesis.line_2: The cheaper path keeps the architecture close to a single-box deployment boundary.

summary.conclusion: The comparison DSL should expose cost span, topology note, and one explicit thesis region.
summary.footer: Use the same family to compare spending, fit, and deployment complexity without changing raw geometry tokens.
